title: Rainy Season Japanese Petit Pois Ramen
description: A citrusy and crumbly Japanese dish featuring petit pois, perfect for the monsoon season, with an ayurvedic-rajasic friendly twist.
instructions: Bring the vegetable broth to a boil in a large pot.
Add the ramen noodles and cook according to the package instructions.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, sesame oil, and grated ginger.
Add the soy sauce mixture to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the petit pois to the pot and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice and cook for 1 minute.
Using a syringe, drop small amounts of the sodium alginate mixture into a bath of cold water to create spheres.
Serve the ramen hot, topped with the petit pois spheres and garnished with sliced green onions.
Repeat the spherification process with the remaining sodium alginate mixture.
